## reference · deceptive-design-dark-pattern-taxonomy
* Deceptive design patterns include: Forced action, Sneaking, Hard to cancel, Preselection, Obstruction, Hidden subscription, Hidden costs, Trick wording, Visual interference, Fake social proof, Fake urgency, Nagging, Confirmshaming, Fake scarcity, Disguised ads, Comparison prevention, Addictive Design, Currency Confusion
* Forced action: 41 laws & rules, 60 enforcements, 23 examples
* Sneaking: 33 laws & rules, 60 enforcements, 18 examples
* Hard to cancel: 46 laws & rules, 23 enforcements, 135 examples
* Preselection: 22 laws & rules, 14 enforcements, 85 examples
* Obstruction: 14 laws & rules, 12 enforcements, 21 examples
* Hidden subscription: 22 laws & rules, 11 enforcements, 28 examples
* Hidden costs: 17 laws & rules, 10 enforcements, 15 examples
* Trick wording: 23 laws & rules, 9 enforcements, 18 examples
* Visual interference: 13 laws & rules, 9 enforcements, 21 examples
* Fake social proof: 8 laws & rules, 7 enforcements, 1 example
* Fake urgency: 8 laws & rules, 4 enforcements, 18 examples
* Nagging: 10 laws & rules, 3 enforcements, 14 examples
* Confirmshaming: 2 laws & rules, 2 enforcements, 21 examples
* Fake scarcity: 4 laws & rules, 1 enforcement, 6 examples
* Disguised ads: 3 laws & rules, 1 enforcement, 8 examples
* Comparison prevention: 1 law, 1 enforcement
* Addictive Design: 3 examples
* Currency Confusion: 2 examples
Sources: https://www.deceptive.design/types
